pattern: Walking each string from the right while counting pending deletions lets you compare the surviving characters without building the edited strings, in linear time and constant space.
hints: Build what each string actually becomes once every backspace is applied, then compare the two results.
A stack reconstructs each edited string cleanly: push a normal character, pop on a backspace if anything remains.
For constant space, scan both strings from the end with two cursors, skipping characters that a run of backspaces would delete, and compare the surviving characters one at a time.

edge_inputs.description: different keystrokes that resolve to the same text
single backspace on a lone letter empties one side
leading backspaces on empty text do nothing
many backspaces erase more than exists
backspace deletes a character mid-string only on one side

stress.description: random mixes of letters and backspaces of varied length
stress.note: heavy '#' density stresses the deletion bookkeeping
